metadata.title: An American dictionary of the English language: intended to exhibit, I. The origin, affinities and primary signification of English words, as far as they have been ascertained. II. The genuine orthography and pronunciation of words, according to general usage, or to just principles of analogy. III. Accurate and discriminating definitions, with numerous authorities and illustrations. To which are prefixed, an introductory dissertation on the origin, history and connection of the languages of Western Asia and of Europe, and a concise grammar of the English language
creator: Webster, Noah, 1758-1843
Howe, Hezekiah
metadata.subject: English language
metadata.copyright-evidence: Evidence reported by ian frederick-rothwell for item americandictiona01websrich on March 20, 2008: no visible notice of copyright; stated date is 1828.

reviews.reviewbody: This is the first year for a Webster's dictionary. A fantastic upload and great resource. It shows that some words today do not have the same meaning as those words from almost 200 years ago. Thank you...
